concerns.text: FR-004 mandates Granger causality tests at lags of 1, 2, and 3 days, but the data alignment in FR-002 and US-2 explicitly normalizes timestamps to 'weekly intervals'. A 1-day lag is mathematically impossible on weekly data. This creates a contradictory, untestable requirement.

SC-002 requires 'p < 0.05 for at least one lag window in the primary correlation test'. Correlation tests (Pearson/Spearman) do not produce 'lag windows'; lag windows are specific to time-series causality tests (Granger). This conflates two different statistical methods, making the success criterion unmeasurable.

FR-001 states the system MUST fetch 'negative news volume' using the 'AVGTONE' metric. AVGTONE is a sentiment score (average tone), not a volume count. The requirement conflates the metric name with the data type, making it impossible to verify if the correct data was fetched without ambiguity.
